data.title: Tetra Tech Non-GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.02, revenue of $1.32B beats by $280M
data.description:

data.title: Innovative Industrial Properties Q1 earnings decline on tenant defaults, but still beat consensus
data.description:
Innovative Industrial Properties (NYSE:IIPR) turned in better-than-expected Q1 earnings and revenue on Wednesday, even as the medical cannabis REIT reported several lease defaults in March.

Q1 normalized FFO per share of $1.84, beating the average analyst estimate of $1.77, fell from $2.03 in Q4 2024 and $1.98 in last year's Q1.

Q1 revenue of $71.7M, exceeding the $70.8M consensus, decreased from $76.7M in the previous quarter and $75.5M a year ago. The Y/Y decline was primarily driven by a decrease of $4.4M on properties leased to PharmaCann due to the tenant’s default, a decrease of $1.6M on properties leased to TILT due to the tenant’s default, and a decrease of $1.0M related to certain properties vacated or sold since Q1 2024.

Expenses of $37.8M rose from $34.7M in Q4 and $33.4M in Q1 2024.

In March Innovative Industrial (NYSE:IIPR) reported lease defaults, including a $2.7M March rent default by PharmaCann, defaults on leases with 4Front, Gold Flora, and TILT (owing $13.1M total), and a $16.1M loan default secured by some California properties.

For the three months ended March 31, 2025, Innovative Industrial (IIPR) applied $5.8M of security deposits for payment of rent on properties leased to PharmaCann, Gold Flora, TILT and Sozo.

In April, the company sold a property in Michigan for $9.0M and provided an interest only, secured loans for $8.5M to the buyer. It also executed a purchase and sale agreement to sell a property in Palm Springs, California, for $2.0M.

Innovative Industrial (IIPR) has re-leased three properties in which rent commencement is contingent on tenants getting the approvals necessary to operate.  As a result, IIPR doesn't expect to recognize rental revenue from those properties until such events take place.

IIPR stock rose 0.5% in after-hours trading.

Conference call on May 8 at 12:00 PM ET.

Earlier, Innovative Industrial Properties FFO of $1.84 beats by $0.07, revenue of $71.7M beats by $0.61M

data.title: Kratos shares dip as cash outflow, unmanned systems loss offset solid bookings
data.description:
Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (NASDAQ:KTOS) on Wednesday slipped 13% after reporting a rise in cash outflow and widening losses in its unmanned systems segment during the first quarter.

Revenue rose 9.2%, or 7.4% organically, from a year earlier to $302.6 million for the three-month period through March, beating the average estimate of $292.3 million among Wall Street analysts.

The company reported net income of $4.5 million, or $0.03 a share, up from $1.3 million, or $0.01 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings of $0.12 a share beat the consensus estimate of $0.09 a share.

Kratos (NASDAQ:KTOS) reported a free cash outflow of $51.8 million for the quarter, compared with a much smaller outflow last year. This was driven by higher inventory levels to support anticipated production ramps, continued capital-intensive investments in unmanned systems and hypersonics and working capital strain from revenue growth.

The defense technology company posted bookings of $365.6 million for the quarter, translating to a consolidated book-to-bill ratio of 1.2 to 1. Total backlog grew to $1.51 billion, up from $1.45 billion at the end of 2024, signaling sustained demand across Kratos' core markets.

Chief Executive Eric DeMarco in a statement noted that improved clarity in federal defense budgets -- including a potential $1 trillion fiscal 2026 national security budget -- is helping unlock programmatic and contractual momentum across Kratos' portfolio.

Segment results

By segment, Government Solutions (KGS) revenues climbed to $239.5 million, up 7.8% organically, driven by double-digit growth in areas like C5ISR, microwave products, and defense rocket systems. KGS delivered $17 million in operating income and $25 million in adjusted EBITDA for the quarter.

Unmanned Systems (KUS) revenues rose 6.2% to $63.1 million, largely from increased target drone sales. However, KUS reported an operating loss of $1.7 million – up from $0.4 million a year ago -- reflecting ongoing margin pressure from fixed-price contracts negotiated before the recent inflationary cycle. The company also cited elevated material, labor and subcontractor costs.

DeMarco said Kratos’ (KTOS) investment in expanding U.S.-based manufacturing capacity, including the upcoming relocation of its Israel-based microwave electronics unit and production scale-ups in hypersonic, propulsion, and small jet engine technologies. He said Kratos’ (KTOS) unique position in mil-spec hardware and secure satellite communications leaves it well-positioned to capitalize on a “generational” recapitalization of the U.S. defense industrial base.

Softer outlook

Looking ahead, Kratos reaffirmed its full-year 2025 revenue guidance of $1.26 billion to $1.285 billion, with adjusted ebitda expected between $112 million and $118 million.

Management acknowledged that the relocation of a key microwave electronics facility in Israel would temporarily reduce productivity and financial results in June, with expectations that revenue and ebitda will rebound in July and August.

The company forecast second-quarter revenues between $300 million and $310 million, with adjusted ebitda of $21 million to $25 million. Because of heavy investment in new facilities and inventory builds for key programs, Kratos (KTOS) expects full-year free cash flow usage of $75 million to $85 million.

data.title: Jackson Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $5.10 beats by $0.17, revenue of $3.75B beats by $1.97B
data.description:
• Jackson Financial press release (NYSE:JXN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $5.10 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $3.75B (vs. -$322M Y/Y) beats by $1.97B.
  • Shares +1.12%.
  • Retail annuity sales1 of $4.0 billion in the first quarter of 2025, up 9% from the first quarter of 2024
    • Variable annuity sales1 of $2.7 billion in the first quarter of 2025, up 9% from the first quarter of 2024, with a more than 40% increase in sales of variable annuities without lifetime benefits
    • Registered index-linked annuity sales of $1.2 billion in the first quarter of 2025, up 3% from the first quarter of 2024
    • Fixed and fixed index annuity sales of $174 million in the first quarter of 2025, up 74% from the first quarter of 2024
  • Net income (loss) attributable to Jackson Financial Inc. common shareholders of $(35) million, or $(0.48) per diluted share in the first quarter of 2025, partially driven by a loss on reinsured business, compared to $784 million, or $9.94 per diluted share in the first quarter of 2024
  • Adjusted operating earnings growth driven in part by a 2% increase in average annuity AUM, from $242 billion in the first quarter of 2024 to $246 billion in the first quarter of 2025, largely due to higher average RILA AUM
  • Robust capital position at the operating company, with total adjusted capital of over $5.2 billion and an estimated risk-based capital ratio at Jackson National Life Insurance Company (JNLIC) of 585%
  • Free cash flow2 in the first quarter of 2025 of $213 million reflecting distributions from our operating company of $240 million. Free cash flow for the 12 months ended March 31, 2025, totaled nearly $1 billion, reflecting distributions from our operating company of nearly $1.1 billion.
  • Returned $231 million to common shareholders in the first quarter of 2025 through $172 million of common share repurchases and $59 million in common dividends
  • Cash and highly liquid securities at the holding company of over $600 million as of March 31, 2025, which was above Jackson’s targeted $250 million minimum liquidity buffer
